title: When to Use a Fake Sneeze vs Awkward Silence
content: We recommend using a Fake Sneeze whenever possible because it creates an immediate, socially acceptable physical barrier, which makes it much more effective than staring blankly while your coworker describes their dream in excruciating detail. You can use a Fake Sneeze for many types of social escapes, including: Avoiding eye contact in a crowded elevator, Interrupting a monologue about cryptocurrency, Escaping a bad date just before the check arrives, or Signaling to your friend that you need rescuing at a party. You should ask yourself: Can I make a convincing achoo sound without actually spraying saliva everywhere? If the answer is yes, then you should choose a Fake Sneeze. On the other hand, a Fake Sneeze is not a good idea if you cannot keep a straight face afterward. Maybe your acting skills are terrible, and the subsequent giggle changes your escape plan into a confession. In that case, you can use Awkward Silence. It will be incredibly painful for everyone involved, but the sheer discomfort will eventually force them to stop talking. Or you can skip interaction entirely and just pretend to get a sudden, urgent phone call.

title: When to Use T-Rex Arms vs Jazz Hands in Photos
content: We recommend using T-Rex Arms (elbows tucked tight to ribs, wrists limp) whenever possible because it minimizes your physical presence, which makes it much safer than accidentally slapping the person next to you in a crowded group selfie. You can use T-Rex Arms for many types of photographic situations, including: Meeting your partner's judgmental parents, Corporate headshots where you don't know what to do with your hands, Standing next to someone way more attractive than you, or Any photo taken before 8:00 AM. You should ask yourself: Can I hold my arms rigidly against my torso like a shy prehistoric carnivore? If the answer is yes, then you should choose T-Rex Arms. On the other hand, T-Rex Arms are not a good idea if you are trying to look fun or spontaneous. Maybe you look too tense, and your stiff posture changes the vibe from happy birthday to hostage situation. In that case, you can use Jazz Hands. It will be incredibly cheesy, but the explosive energy will distract everyone from your forced smile. Or you can skip posing entirely and just offer to take the photo so you aren't in it.

title: When to Use Interpretive Dance vs Charades on Zoom
content: We recommend using Interpretive Dance whenever possible because your full-body movements convey deep emotional truths, which makes it much more artistic than frantically pointing at a coffee mug when your microphone is muted. You can use Interpretive Dance for many types of video call mishaps, including: Explaining that your internet connection is unstable through fluid arm waves, Expressing existential dread during the quarterly budget meeting, Signaling that your cat is currently vomiting behind the computer, or Physically representing the concept of synergy. You should ask yourself: Can I flail my limbs rhythmically without knocking over my desk lamp? If the answer is yes, then you should choose Interpretive Dance. On the other hand, Interpretive Dance is not a good idea if you cannot stand up fully in your camera frame. Maybe you are only wearing professional clothes from the waist up, and the sudden movement reveals your pajama pants. In that case, you can use Charades. It will be frustratingly slow, but holding up two fingers to indicate two syllables is safer for your professional reputation. Or you can skip communicating entirely and just hang up and blame technology.

title: When to Use Sunglasses Inside vs Avoiding Eye Contact
content: We recommend using Sunglasses Inside whenever possible because it creates an aura of mystery and coolness, which makes it much more stylish than stare-down at your own shoes while walking through the grocery store. You can use Sunglasses Inside for many types of public evasion, including: Hiding a massive hangover at brunch, Pretending you are a minor celebrity trying to avoid paparazzi, Buying questionable items at the pharmacy, or Ignoring someone you vaguely know from high school. You should ask yourself: Can I wear these shades without tripping over furniture because it's actually quite dark in here? If the answer is yes, then you should choose Sunglasses Inside. On the other hand, Sunglasses Inside is not a good idea if you cannot handle people judging you. Maybe you aren't confident enough, and looking like a confused blues musician changes your anxiety levels for the worse. In that case, you can use Avoiding Eye Contact. It will make you look incredibly shifty, but staring intensely at a can of beans is less conspicuous. Or you can skip the store entirely and just order delivery at a 300 percent markup.

title: When to Use the Hover-Hand vs An Actual Hug
content: We recommend using the Hover-Hand whenever possible because maintaining a solid four inches of air gap between your palm and their shoulder shows immense respect for personal boundaries, which makes it much less risky than misinterpreting the level of intimacy in a greeting. You can use the Hover-Hand for many types of awkward encounters, including: Greeting your boss's spouse at the holiday party, Taking photos with cosplayers at a convention, Interacting with distant cousins you haven't seen in a decade, or Congratulating someone you secretly dislike. You should ask yourself: Can I hold my arm suspended in mid-air until my deltoid muscle starts to spasm? If the answer is yes, then you should choose the Hover-Hand. On the other hand, the Hover-Hand is not a good idea if the photographic evidence will haunt you forever. Maybe someone takes a picture, and your floating hand looks deeply creepy on Instagram. In that case, you can use An Actual Hug. It will be terrifyingly vulnerable, but brief physical contact is better than looking like a malfunctioning robot. Or you can skip touching entirely and just offer a crisp, formal salute.

title: When to Use Lip-Syncing vs Singing Happy Birthday Off-Key
content: We recommend using Lip-Syncing whenever possible because your complete lack of vocal talent remains a secret, which makes it much more pleasant for everyone than having your voice crack on the high note of the third verse. You can use Lip-Syncing for many types of mandatory celebrations, including: Office birthday parties for people you barely know, Crowded restaurants where the waiters are already yelling, Zoom celebrations where the audio lag is terrible anyway, or Any time you are sober. You should ask yourself: Can I mouth the word cha-cha-cha convincingly without actually making a sound? If the answer is yes, then you should choose Lip-Syncing. On the other hand, Lip-Syncing is not a good idea if you cannot blend in with the crowd. Maybe there are only three other people singing, and your silence changes the volume drastically. In that case, you can use Singing Off-Key. It will be embarrassing, but your enthusiastic, terrible howling will show you care. Or you can skip the song entirely and just hide in the bathroom until the cake is served.

title: When to Use Cargo Shorts vs A Fanny Pack
content: We recommend using Cargo Shorts whenever possible because the sheer volume of pockets allows you to carry a small hardware store on your person, which makes it much more rugged than having a small bag strapped to your midsection like a confused tourist. You can use Cargo Shorts for many types of utility situations, including: Smuggling snacks into a movie theater, Carrying 47 loose screws you might need someday, Holding your wallet, keys, phone, and a backup phone, or Dads at a theme park. You should ask yourself: Can I walk normally despite having fifteen pounds of gear swinging around my knees? If the answer is yes, then you should choose Cargo Shorts. On the other hand, Cargo Shorts are not a good idea if you are trying to look remotely fashionable in this decade. Maybe you are on a date, and the baggy silhouette changes your appeal entirely. In that case, you can use A Fanny Pack. It will be worn ironically across your chest, but at least your keys won't hit your ankles. Or you can skip carrying things entirely and just force your partner to hold everything in their purse.

title: When to Use Socks with Sandals vs Crocs
content: We recommend using Socks with Sandals whenever possible because it signals a complete and total surrender to comfort over aesthetics, which makes it much more honest than trying to make plastic clogs look trendy. You can use Socks with Sandals for many types of low-effort days, including: Taking out the trash in November, Quick trips to the gas station for milk, Embarrassing your teenagers in public, or BBQing in your own backyard where no one can judge you. You should ask yourself: Can I wear white tube socks with brown leather straps without feeling a deep sense of shame? If the answer is yes, then you should choose Socks with Sandals. On the other hand, Socks with Sandals is not a good idea if it is actively raining. Maybe there are puddles everywhere, and soggy wool changes your comfort level to zero. In that case, you can use Crocs. They will still look ridiculous, but at least the water drains out of the little holes. Or you can skip footwear entirely and just stay in bed.

title: When to Use Speaking Louder in English vs Learning the Language
content: We recommend using Speaking Louder in English whenever possible because increasing the decibels helps express your confusion, which makes it much faster than having to spend months on Duolingo learning basic grammar. You can use Speaking Louder in English for many types of international travel situations, including: Ordering coffee in Paris and receiving a blank stare, Asking for directions to the bathroom in Tokyo, Negotiating taxi fares in Rome, or Expressing your allergy to peanuts in Bangkok. You should ask yourself: Can I repeat the phrase WHERE IS THE TRAIN very slowly while gesturing wildly? If the answer is yes, then you should choose Speaking Louder in English. On the other hand, Speaking Louder in English is not a good idea if you want to not look like an ignorant tourist. Maybe the locals are getting annoyed, and your volume changes their helpfulness to hostility. In that case, you can use Learning the Language. It will be impossibly difficult, but knowing how to say please might help. Or you can skip talking entirely and just use Google Translate on your phone until the battery dies.

title: When to Use Reply All Regret vs Ghosting the Email Chain
content: We recommend experiencing Reply All Regret whenever possible because the immediate adrenaline rush of realizing you just sent a snarky comment to the CEO keeps you feeling alive, which makes it much more exciting than having a boring, functional inbox. You can use Reply All Regret for many types of corporate communications, including: Company-wide announcements about the new refrigerator policy, RSVPing no to the optional holiday party with a sarcastic excuse, Accidentally attaching your resume to a team update, or Gossiping about Karen in accounting, to Karen in accounting. You should ask yourself: Can I hit the send button before my brain registers that there are 400 recipients? If the answer is yes, then you should choose Reply All Regret. On the other hand, Reply All Regret is not a good idea if you actually want to keep your job. Maybe the email was highly sensitive, and your mistake changes your employment status. In that case, you can use Ghosting the Email Chain. It will be unprofessional, but pretending you never saw the message is safer than engaging. Or you can skip email entirely and throw your laptop into the nearest river.

title: When to Use Holding It In vs Blaming the Dog
content: We recommend using Holding It In whenever possible because the sheer physical fortitude required builds character, which makes it much more noble than having an innocent animal take the fall for your digestive issues. You can use Holding It In for many types of quiet situations, including: Job interviews in small, unventilated offices, First dates during the silent part of a movie, Meeting your partner's parents for a fancy dinner, or Crowded elevators stuck between floors. You should ask yourself: Can I clench my muscles until my face turns slightly purple? If the answer is yes, then you should choose Holding It In. On the other hand, Holding It In is not a good idea if the pressure becomes critical. Maybe you ate too much dairy, and the biological imperative changes your ability to remain seated. In that case, you can use Blaming the Dog. It will be a lie, but looking at the family pet with disgust is a classic deflection tactic. Or you can skip subterfuge entirely and just leave the room immediately without explanation.

title: When to Use Pretending to Text vs Staring at the Floor
content: We recommend using Pretending to Text whenever possible because it makes you look busy and important, which makes it much less pathetic than having to study the linoleum pattern while waiting for an appointment. You can use Pretending to Text for many types of awkward waiting periods, including: Standing alone at a party where you don't know anyone, Waiting for a friend who is 30 minutes late, Sharing an elevator with your boss, or Sitting in the dentist's waiting room. You should ask yourself: Can I scroll aimlessly through my phone's settings menu while furrowing my brow seriously? If the answer is yes, then you should choose Pretending to Text. On the other hand, Pretending to Text is not a good idea if your phone battery is dead. Maybe the screen is clearly black, and your furious tapping changes how sane you appear to others. In that case, you can use Staring at the Floor. It will be boring, but counting the tiles is a safe, neutral activity. Or you can skip waiting entirely and just leave.

title: When to Use 2-in-1 Shampoo and Conditioner vs Having Standards
content: We recommend using 2-in-1 Shampoo and Conditioner whenever possible because the thirty seconds you save in the shower adds up over a lifetime, which makes it much more efficient than having a bathroom cluttered with multiple bottles. You can use 2-in-1 Shampoo and Conditioner for many types of hair care routines, including: Gym showers where you just want to leave, Camping trips where hygiene is already questionable, When you are late for work and don't care what you look like, or If you are a teenage boy. You should ask yourself: Can I accept hair that is simultaneously greasy and dry? If the answer is yes, then you should choose 2-in-1 Shampoo and Conditioner. On the other hand, 2-in-1 Shampoo and Conditioner is not a good idea if you want your hair to look good. Maybe you have a date later, and the frizz changes your confidence level. In that case, you can use Having Standards. It will take longer to wash your hair properly, but the result will be actual cleanliness. Or you can skip washing entirely and just wear a hat for three days.

title: When to Use Eating Over the Sink vs Using a Plate
content: We recommend using Eating Over the Sink whenever possible because the crumbs fall directly into the drain, which makes it much faster than having to wash a dish, dry it, and put it away later. You can use Eating Over the Sink for many types of culinary experiences, including: Consuming a cold slice of pizza at 2:00 AM, Eating a messy peach over the summer, Devouring a sandwich in three bites before leaving the house, or Hiding snacks from your children. You should ask yourself: Can I hunch over the faucet like a scavenging raccoon? If the answer is yes, then you should choose Eating Over the Sink. On the other hand, Eating Over the Sink is not a good idea if you are serving guests. Maybe you are hosting a dinner party, and your lack of table manners changes the ambiance. In that case, you can use Using a Plate. It will require effort to clean up, but civilization depends on these small rituals. Or you can skip dishes entirely and just order takeout and eat straight from the styrofoam container.

title: When to Use The Irish Goodbye vs The 45-Minute Doorway Chat
content: We recommend using The Irish Goodbye (leaving without telling anyone) whenever possible because you simply vanish into the night like a mysterious phantom, which makes it much more efficient than having to hug twenty different people you barely know. You can use The Irish Goodbye for many types of social gatherings, including: Crowded house parties where the host is already drunk, Networking events that have gone on too long, Family reunions when politics gets brought up, or Bad dates. You should ask yourself: Can I slip out the back door without being spotted by the chatty extrovert near the exit? If the answer is yes, then you should choose The Irish Goodbye. On the other hand, The Irish Goodbye is not a good idea if it is a small dinner party. Maybe there are only four people left, and your sudden absence changes the mood to confused panic. In that case, you can use The 45-Minute Doorway Chat. It will be exhausting as you slowly inch toward the door while still talking, but it is polite. Or you can skip the party entirely and stay home.

title: When to Use Wearing Pajamas to the Store vs Getting Dressed
content: We recommend using Wearing Pajamas to the Store whenever possible because it signals to the world that you have given up today, which makes it much more liberating than having to put on pants with a zipper. You can use Wearing Pajamas to the Store for many types of errands, including: Buying cold medicine at 6:00 AM, Emergency late-night ice cream runs, Returning a Redbox movie before the deadline, or Driving through a fast-food window. You should ask yourself: Can I wear these plaid flannel bottoms in public without making eye contact with anyone I know? If the answer is yes, then you should choose Wearing Pajamas to the Store. On the other hand, Wearing Pajamas to the Store is not a good idea if you might run into your boss. Maybe you work at the store you are going to, and your fleece onesie changes your professional standing. In that case, you can use Getting Dressed. It will be awful to put on real clothes, but society demands it. Or you can skip the errand entirely and just suffer without the item you needed.

title: When to Use Drinking Straight from the Carton vs A Glass
content: We recommend using Drinking Straight from the Carton whenever possible because it is the most direct route between the beverage and your mouth, which makes it much faster than having to locate clean glassware. You can use Drinking Straight from the Carton for many types of hydration needs, including: 3:00 AM thirst emergencies, Finishing the last sip of orange juice, Testing if the milk has gone bad, or When you live alone and have no shame. You should ask yourself: Can I chug this liquid before anyone walks into the kitchen and judges me? If the answer is yes, then you should choose Drinking Straight from the Carton. On the other hand, Drinking Straight from the Carton is not a good idea if you share a fridge with roommates. Maybe they saw you do it, and your backwash changes their willingness to live with you. In that case, you can use A Glass. It will create more dishes, but it is sanitary. Or you can skip drinking entirely and just sit in dehydration.

title: When to Use a Fake Number vs Polite Rejection
content: We recommend using a Fake Number whenever possible because it provides an immediate escape route with zero confrontation, which makes it much easier than having to explain to a stranger why you aren't interested in their mixtape. You can use a Fake Number for many types of unwanted advances, including: Creepy guys at closing time in a bar, People trying to sign you up for multi-level marketing schemes, Persistent salespeople at the mall kiosk, or Someone asking for donations on the street. You should ask yourself: Can I recite 867-5309 with a straight face before running away? If the answer is yes, then you should choose a Fake Number. On the other hand, a Fake Number is not a good idea if they call it right in front of you. Maybe their phone rings in your pocket because you gave them your real number by mistake, and the ringing changes the situation to maximum awkwardness. In that case, you can use Polite Rejection. It will be uncomfortable, but honesty is sometimes necessary. Or you can skip interaction entirely and fake a sudden fainting spell.

title: When to Use Laughing at Jokes You Do Not Get vs Asking For a Repeat
content: We recommend using Laughing at Jokes You Do Not Get whenever possible because a generic chuckle keeps the conversation moving, which makes it much smoother than having the person explain the punchline three times until it is not funny anymore. You can use Laughing at Jokes You Do Not Get for many types of social interactions, including: Noisy bars where you cannot hear anything anyway, Talking to your boss when they think they are a comedian, Meeting your partner's eccentric grandfather, or When you zoned out for the entire setup of the story. You should ask yourself: Can I produce a convincing hahaha while my eyes remain completely dead inside? If the answer is yes, then you should choose Laughing at Jokes You Do Not Get. On the other hand, Laughing at Jokes You Do Not Get is not a good idea if the joke was actually a question. Maybe they are waiting for an answer, and your laughter changes their opinion of your sanity. In that case, you can use Asking For a Repeat. It will be humiliating to admit you were not listening, but it clarifies the situation. Or you can skip responding entirely and point behind them yelling what is that? before running away.

title: When to Use Pushing a Pull Door vs Reading Signs
content: We recommend using Pushing a Pull Door whenever possible because assertively slamming your body into glass shows confidence, which makes it much more alpha than having to slow down and process basic textual information. You can use Pushing a Pull Door for many types of entryways, including: Coffee shops when you are in a rush for caffeine, Office buildings where you want to make a dramatic entrance, Restaurants when you are hangry, or Any door that looks slightly confusing. You should ask yourself: Can I handle the physical recoil when the door refuses to budge? If the answer is yes, then you should choose Pushing a Pull Door. On the other hand, Pushing a Pull Door is not a good idea if people are watching from the other side. Maybe they are laughing at you, and the embarrassment changes your desire to enter the building. In that case, you can use Reading Signs. It will take a fraction of a second, but it prevents you from looking like a caveman. Or you can skip entering entirely and just walk away pretending you meant to do that.

title: When to Use Dry Shampoo for 5 Days vs Washing Your Hair
content: We recommend using Dry Shampoo for 5 Days whenever possible because spraying white powder onto your roots takes thirty seconds, which makes it much easier than the forty-five-minute ordeal of washing, drying, and styling your hair. You can use Dry Shampoo for 5 Days for many types of weeks, including: Finals week when you live in the library, When you are depressed and cannot leave bed, Camping festivals, or When your water heater breaks. You should ask yourself: Can I pull my hair into a messy bun to hide the fact that it is mostly aerosol powder? If the answer is yes, then you should choose Dry Shampoo for 5 Days. On the other hand, Dry Shampoo for 5 Days is not a good idea if your scalp starts to itch uncontrollably. Maybe you have visible flakes of starch on your shoulders, and the look changes from chic messy to health code violation. In that case, you can use Washing Your Hair. It will be an exhausting chore, but hygiene is important. Or you can skip washing entirely and just wear a beanie until next month.

title: When to Use Eating Cereal from the Box vs A Bowl and Milk
content: We recommend using Eating Cereal from the Box whenever possible because grabbing handfuls of dry cereal like a toddler is immediate gratification, which makes it much faster than having to assemble a balanced breakfast. You can use Eating Cereal from the Box for many types of snacking needs, including: Watching cartoons on Saturday morning, Late-night munchies, Road trips where crumbs do not matter, or When you have run out of clean bowls. You should ask yourself: Can I tilt the bag into my mouth without spilling half of it onto the couch? If the answer is yes, then you should choose Eating Cereal from the Box. On the other hand, Eating Cereal from the Box is not a good idea if you are terribly thirsty. Maybe the dry crunch is cutting the roof of your mouth, and the lack of hydration changes the enjoyment factor. In that case, you can use A Bowl and Milk. It will require effort, but the cereal will be soft. Or you can skip the bowl entirely and just pour milk directly into the cereal bag.

title: When to Use Duct Tape vs Actual Repairs
content: We recommend using Duct Tape whenever possible because wrapping silver adhesive around a problem is deeply satisfying, which makes it much cheaper than calling a professional plumber, mechanic, or contractor. You can use Duct Tape for many types of broken things, including: Leaky garden hoses, Car bumpers hanging off after a fender bender, Ripped camping tents, or Shoes that are falling apart. You should ask yourself: Can I ignore the fact that this is a severe fire hazard if it holds together for another week? If the answer is yes, then you should choose Duct Tape. On the other hand, Duct Tape is not a good idea if the structure is load-bearing. Maybe you are taping a ladder leg back together, and gravity changes your mind about safety protocols. In that case, you can use Actual Repairs. It will cost money, but you will not get injured. Or you can skip repairs entirely and just throw the broken item in the trash.

title: When to Use Forgot My Wallet vs Paying Your Share
content: We recommend using Forgot My Wallet whenever possible because the sudden look of panic as you pat your pockets is a great acting exercise, which makes it much more profitable than actually spending your own money on dinner. You can use Forgot My Wallet for many types of financial transactions, including: First dates that are not going well, Group dinners where everyone ordered expensive drinks except you, Taxi rides with friends, or Surprise birthday parties you did not want to attend. You should ask yourself: Can I promise to Venmo them later with zero intention of ever doing so? If the answer is yes, then you should choose Forgot My Wallet. On the other hand, Forgot My Wallet is not a good idea if they know you use Apple Pay on your phone. Maybe they point at the phone in your hand, and your lie changes into obvious cheapness. In that case, you can use Paying Your Share. It will hurt your bank account, but you will keep your friends. Or you can skip paying entirely and dash out of the restaurant before the bill comes.

title: When to Use Sleeping on Top of a Made Bed vs Unmaking It
content: We recommend using Sleeping on Top of a Made Bed whenever possible because you do not have to remake it in the morning, which makes it much lazier than having to deal with tangled sheets and duvets. You can use Sleeping on Top of a Made Bed for many types of rest, including: Afternoon naps, When you are too drunk to figure out how covers work, Staying in a hotel and afraid of the sheets, or When you made the bed perfectly for social media and do not want to ruin it. You should ask yourself: Can I sleep comfortably under a thin throw blanket while freezing? If the answer is yes, then you should choose Sleeping on Top of a Made Bed. On the other hand, Sleeping on Top of a Made Bed is not a good idea if it is winter. Maybe the temperature drops, and hypothermia changes your priorities regarding bed-making. In that case, you can use Unmaking It. It will take ten seconds to pull back the covers, and you will be warm. Or you can skip the bed entirely and just sleep on the floor.

title: When to Use a Sock as a Coffee Filter vs Going Without Caffeine
content: We recommend using a Sock as a Coffee Filter whenever possible because desperate times call for desperate measures, which makes it much better than facing the existential dread of a morning without coffee. You can use a Sock as a Coffee Filter for many types of emergencies, including: Camping trips where you forgot the equipment, Moving day when everything is packed, The apocalypse, or College dorm life. You should ask yourself: Can I find a relatively clean athletic sock that does not smell too much like foot? If the answer is yes, then you should choose a Sock as a Coffee Filter. On the other hand, a Sock as a Coffee Filter is not a good idea if you have guests. Maybe they see you pouring hot water through footwear, and their judgment changes their opinion of your coffee. In that case, you can use Going Without Caffeine. It will lead to a massive headache and irritability, but you will maintain some dignity. Or you can skip brewing entirely and just chew on the coffee grounds.

title: When to Use Nodding Knowingly vs Admitting You Were Not Listening
content: We recommend using Nodding Knowingly whenever possible because a slow, thoughtful nod implies you understand complex concepts, which makes it much smarter than admitting you have been daydreaming about lunch for the last ten minutes. You can use Nodding Knowingly for many types of lectures, including: Your partner explaining the plot of a movie you have not seen, Your boss detailing the quarterly strategy, A mechanic explaining what is wrong with your transmission, or Anyone talking about niche trends. You should ask yourself: Can I throw in an occasional hmm or exactly without being asked a direct question? If the answer is yes, then you should choose Nodding Knowingly. On the other hand, Nodding Knowingly is not a good idea if they suddenly ask for your input. Maybe they say what do you think we should do? and your blank stare changes the dynamic instantly. In that case, you can use Admitting You Were Not Listening. It will be embarrassing, but honest. Or you can skip answering entirely and fake a sudden coughing fit until they leave.

title: When to Use Using a Banana as a Phone vs Ignoring the Call
content: We recommend using Using a Banana as a Phone whenever possible because it is an undeniable power move that confuses everyone around you, which makes it much funnier than simply letting your phone go to voicemail. You can use Using a Banana as a Phone for many types of social disruptions, including: Boring business meetings, First dates that are going poorly, Crowded elevators, or Trying to look busy in a grocery store. You should ask yourself: Can I hold this fruit to my ear and shout BUY! BUY! SELL! without breaking character? If the answer is yes, then you should choose Using a Banana as a Phone. On the other hand, Using a Banana as a Phone is not a good idea if you are actually hungry. Maybe you eat the phone mid-conversation, and the potassium changes your priorities. In that case, you can use Ignoring the Call. It will be boring, but you get to eat the banana in peace. Or you can skip the fruit entirely and just yell into your empty hand.

title: When to Use Microwave Burritos vs Actual Cooking
content: We recommend using Microwave Burritos whenever possible because the radioactive heat from the microwave cooks them in two minutes, which makes them much faster than having to chop vegetables and dirty a pan. You can use Microwave Burritos for many types of meal emergencies, including: 1:00 AM gaming sessions, When you are too depressed to make a sandwich, The day before grocery shopping day, or Hangovers. You should ask yourself: Can I accept a food item that is molten lava on the ends and frozen solid in the middle? If the answer is yes, then you should choose Microwave Burritos. On the other hand, Microwave Burritos are not a good idea if you value your digestive health. Maybe the sheer amount of sodium changes your blood pressure immediately. In that case, you can use Actual Cooking. It will take effort and create dishes, but it might be edible. Or you can skip eating entirely and just go to sleep hungry.

title: When to Use Using a Spoon to Cut Things vs Washing a Knife
content: We recommend using Using a Spoon to Cut Things whenever possible because it saves you from having to wash a sharp knife later, which makes it much safer than risking a cut while doing dishes. You can use Using a Spoon to Cut Things for many types of soft foods, including: Avocados, Bananas, Very soft brownies, or cooked chicken if you press really hard. You should ask yourself: Can I saw through this food item with a dull, rounded edge until it looks like it was mangled by a wild animal? If the answer is yes, then you should choose Using a Spoon to Cut Things. On the other hand, Using a Spoon to Cut Things is not a good idea if you are eating steak. Maybe the meat is tough, and your futile sawing changes your dinner into a workout. In that case, you can use Washing a Knife. It will take ten seconds, but you will be able to eat properly. Or you can skip cutlery entirely and just pick up the food with your hands.
